# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

Use this section to tell people about which versions of your project are currently being supported with security updates.

| Version | Supported          |
| ------- | ------------------ |
| 1.0.x   | :white_check_mark: |
| < 1.0.0 | :x:                |

## Reporting a Vulnerability

We take the security of our project seriously.

If you believe you've found a security vulnerability in our project, please follow these steps:

1. **Do not disclose the vulnerability publicly**
2. **Email us at [YOUR-EMAIL]** with details about the vulnerability
3. Include the following information in your report:
   - Type of issue (e.g., buffer overflow, SQL injection, cross-site scripting, etc.)
   - Full paths of source file(s) related to the vulnerability
   - The location of the affected source code (tag/branch/commit or direct URL)
   - Any special configuration required to reproduce the issue
   - Step-by-step instructions to reproduce the issue
   - Proof-of-concept or exploit code (if possible)
   - Impact of the issue, including how an attacker might exploit the issue

## Preferred Languages

We prefer all communications to be in English.

## Response Policy

- We aim to acknowledge receipt of vulnerability reports within 48 hours
- We will provide regular updates about our progress
- We will notify you when the vulnerability has been fixed

Thank you for helping keep our project and our users secure!
